arts.ttl: Manchester United handed new Marcus Rashford future verdict as Cristiano Ronaldo point made
arts.athr: newsdesk@men-news.co.uk (Thomas Fair, Manchester Evening News readers)
arts.sum: Speculation over Marcus Rashford and his future at Old Trafford remains at fever pitch after the forward revealed that he's ready to leave Man United.

arts.ttl: Sir Jim Ratcliffe makes £79m Man Utd decision but it's bad news for Ruben Amorim
arts.sum: Sir Jim Ratcliffe has increased his stake in Manchester United, pouring nearly £80million into the club, but that is unlikely to end in Ruben Amorim's transfer kitty

arts.ttl: “He wants to stay” – Mixed messages as Tottenham player, eager to fight under Ange and then reassess in summer
arts.sum: Richarlison wants to stay at Tottenham in January and fight for a place under Ange Postecoglou before then reassessing his future in the summer. That’s according to journalist Saisa Simplicio, who says a move is not on the cards in January despite reports to the contrary. Richarlison has found himself out of the picture again The post “He wants to stay” – Mixed messages as Tottenham player, eager to fight under Ange and then reassess in summer appeared first on Sport Witness.

arts.ttl: 'Too easy' - Ruben Amorim has work to do to solve Manchester United's biggest weakness
arts.sum: Manchester United have struggled to defend corners time and again this season

arts.ttl: Amad suggests Man United have a better 'leader' than Bruno Fernandes
arts.sum: The decision to appoint Bruno Fernandes as Manchester United captain has been criticised ever since the get-go - and now one of his teammates has even suggested that he isn't the best leader at the club

arts.ttl: Ratcliffe increases Manchester United stake after paying agreed investment
arts.sum: Investment will not be used to bolster current squad Club now part of wider sporting group that Ineos owns Sir Jim Ratcliffe has increased his stake in Manchester United after injecting a further £79.3m ($100m) promised when he bought his minority stake last year. The British billionaire now owns 28.94% of the club, up from 27.7%. The latest investment is set to help fund improvements off the pitch rather than help bolster Ruben Amorim’s squad during January’s transfer window. When Ratcliffe bought into United a year ago, the deal included a guarantee that he would put £238m ($300m) into infrastructure and this is the final instalment of those payments, which the US Securities and Exchange Commission confirmed was paid on Wednesday. arts.ttl: Ruben Amorim still has to solve biggest Alejandro Garnacho problem at Manchester United
arts.sum: The recently-appointed head coach raised eyebrows when he dropped Alejandro Garnacho and Marcus Rashford for what turned out to be a memorable first Manchester derby in the dugout.
